candidate_reviews.reason: The frozen tests assert architecture-analysis language behavior, not merely obsolete package structure. These laws need successor ownership or an explicit later harness extraction; superseding them now would erase negative AST-policy cases.
The frozen tests assert dependency and transport ownership laws. Some package names are stale, but the behavioral boundary remains active and needs successor evidence rather than supersession.
Complete-suite and package-installation validations remain real release-candidate obligations. They are not obsolete structural assertions.

summary: No required-core frozen law was safely superseded in #732. The manifest language was hardened so future supersessions must state the obsolete assumption, replacement, and negative-case disposition.
